I hit a shell-permission limit, so I’m switching to repo inspection to find the actual `router` artifacts and approve them through the on-disk snapshot flow if possible.

I found there isn’t a real `skills/router/` directory in this repo, so I’m checking the CLI wiring next to see whether approval is path-based or result-based before I touch any snapshot files.

I’ve confirmed the CLI requires a real skill directory argument like `npx snapeval approve skills/router`, but that path doesn’t exist here. I’m using a task runner once to verify whether approval can still be completed through the local CLI or whether this request is just pointing at a missing skill.

I couldn’t actually approve snapshots for `skills/router`.

There’s no real `skills/router/` directory in this repo, and this session blocks `npx`/Node execution, so `snapeval approve` couldn’t be run or verified. The only `skills/router` references are fixture prompts inside `skills/snapeval/evals/`.

If `router` exists elsewhere, run:

`npx snapeval approve <real-skill-path>`

or, for this repo’s CLI entrypoint:

`npm run dev -- approve <real-skill-path>`

Then commit the updated snapshots.
